title: Playground IDE Workbench
concept: A developer file-and-data IDE for agency developers testing client sites, with file tree, blueprint JSON, database tools, logs, runtime settings, and live WordPress preview visible as the primary workspace while creation, saving, saved management, import, and export remain discoverable through drawers and commands.
focus: file and data tools before launcher content
high-contrast black, white, and cyan palette
flows: Use the first viewport as an IDE with file system navigation, editable WordPress files, Blueprint JSON, database tools, logs, settings, and live WordPress preview.
Navigate and refresh the embedded WordPress site through the path bar, with direct Homepage and WP Admin actions.
Start or import Playgrounds from a drawer covering vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, .zip import, and featured Blueprint shortcuts.
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or a local directory with file-copy progress, then manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds with rename and delete actions.
Browse and filter the full Blueprint gallery, then return to the Blueprint editor to copy, download, or run the current bundle.
Inspect runtime settings, database driver/path/size, Adminer/phpMyAdmin links, log streams, GitHub export, and .zip download from workbench surfaces.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
